channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Preparing for the impact of AI in government contracting projects
item.description: The impact of artificial intelligence in government contracting projects is transformative as it creates new avenues to be better and stand out. The post Preparing for the impact of AI in government contracting projects first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Navigating the pressures facing CIOs
item.description: According to consulting firm Korn Ferry,[1] the average CIO is in their role for just 4.6 years. That’s shorter than most of their boardroom peers and significantly less than CEOs’ average tenure of 6.9 years.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The US agency that monitors weather will cut another 1,000 jobs, AP sources say
item.description: Multiple sources tell The Associated Press that the Trump Administration is starting another round of job cuts — this one more than 1,000 — at the nation’s weather, ocean and fisheries agency.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What the breakup of Google could mean for Apple and other tech companies
item.description: Alphabet may have hoped that Donald Trump winning the White House would quell talk of a Google breakup, but that doesn’t seem likely.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Future PlayStation games could have AI-powered characters, if this leaked prototype of Aloy is anything to go by
item.description: AI-powered characters could feature in future PlayStation games, and the Horizon series could be the testbed with an AI Aloy.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Asus might have just changed the display game for good with three new air-purifying monitors - and one of them is ultrawide
item.description: Asus' new monitors may not include any significant improvements to their display quality, but the Taiwanese company is bringing enhancements to indoor air quality in a unique way.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ransomware gangs allegedly hit two major US healthcare firms, 300,000 patients have data stolen
item.description: Sunflower Medical Group and Community Care Alliance confirmed suffering a cyberattack as Rhysida claims responsibility.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Dangerous malware targets you through fake loan app
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says a malware app called SpyLend is posing as a financial tool, hitting 100,000-plus downloads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How AI dramatically boosts efficiency of B2B order processing
item.description: With an aging, shrinking workforce, it’s become harder and harder for B2B organizations to staff their sales teams, even while customers demand faster fulfillment times and access to their suppliers across multiple channels.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New Measures to Combat Illegal Tourist Rentals in Ibiza
item.description: Ibiza authorities announce a new step in the fight against illegal tourist rentals, streamlining inspections and penalties through data integration.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump says he’s buying ‘brand new’ Tesla to support Musk
item.description: President Trump said he would be buying a “brand new” Tesla to support his senior adviser Elon Musk, as the stock market shows shares of the electric car company tumbling.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft cheat sheets: Dive into Windows and Office apps
item.description: Need to get up to speed on the latest features in Excel? Wrestling with an old version of Word? Finding your way around Windows 11 or looking to get more out of Windows 10? Computerworld’s cheat sheets are easy-to-use guides to help you navigate Microsoft’s core productivity software.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta, Apple Face Modest Fines for Alleged DMA Breaches - TechRadar Pro
item.description: The European Union's Digital Markets Act (DMA) could see Meta and Apple facing fines for alleged breaches, but it's believed that the EU may prioritize compliance over hefty penalties.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tesla stock plummets, but its sky-high valuation persists. Here’s why
item.description: Tesla’s stock has dropped by nearly half in three months. Even so, investors are still debating whether Elon Musk’s electric-vehicle maker remains overpriced.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: ServiceNow to pay $2.85B for Moveworks’ AI tools
item.description: ServiceNow announced plans to purchase Moveworks, its front-end AI assistant and enterprise search technology for $2.85 billion in cash and stock.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: TikTok’s ‘recession brunette’ trend signals tough economic times
item.description: Noticed all the blondes going back to their natural hair color lately? As much as many try to claim it’s because of a “hair health journey,” other factors seem to be at play here. “How’s the economy?” one TikTok user asks in a viral video.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Cancer Treatment Breakthrough: USC Study Reveals Gene Mutations Key to Personalized Care
item.description: A landmark study from the University of Southern California could hold the key to improving the efficacy of cancer treatment. Researchers found over 700 genetic alterations that directly impacted survival rates and created an AI tool to predict lung cancer patient responses to immunotherapy.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft previews AI chat template for .NET
item.description: Microsoft is previewing a .NET web app template intended to enable quick development of AI-powered chat applications. Part of an ongoing effort to make AI development with .NET easier to discover and use, the AI Chat Web App template was announced March 6.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Duckduckgo launches its version of an AI search assistant
item.description: After nearly two years of intensive beta testing, Duckduckgo has launched the live version of the AI assistant previously known as Duck Assist. Similar to Google’s AI Overviews, the AI Assistant can provide quick answers to users’ questions, such as why Pluto is no longer classified as a planet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Drones are already transforming business
item.description: When you hear the word “drone,” what comes to mind? Most people think of fun consumer recreational copters that capture incredible video from the sky. Others think of military drones that spy on battlefields and drop bombs. It’s true that drones are transforming photography and war.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify ages
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah on Wednesday became the first state to pass legislation requiring app stores to verify users’ ages and get parental consent for minors to download apps to their devices. The bill headed to the desk of Gov.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Georgia lawmakers push to ban automated speed cameras near schools
item.description: DECATUR, Ga. (AP) — Outside Beacon Hill Middle School in the Atlanta suburb of Decatur, like along hundreds of roadsides across Georgia, the unblinking eye of a camera tickets drivers who speed through a school zone.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4
